sanskrit:
तस्माच्छास्त्रं प्रमाणं ते कार्याकार्यव्यवस्थितौ।
ज्ञात्वा शास्त्रविधानोक्तं कर्म कर्तुमिहार्हसि॥
transliteration:
tasmāc chāstraṃ pramāṇaṃ te kāryākārya-vyavasthitau |
jñātvā śāstra-vidhānoktaṃ karma kartum ihārhasi ||
literal_meaning: Therefore, for you the śāstra is the authority in determining what is to be done and what is not to be done; having known what is prescribed by the ordinance of the śāstra, you should act here (in this world).
contextual_meaning: After mapping the demonic traits that hijack decision-making, Kṛṣṇa gives Arjuna a cognitive anchor: use the internalized ‘manual’ (śāstra) as the external superego that arbitrates right/wrong, then act—turning moral anxiety into regulated behavior.
psychological_state: Post-choice clarity: the paralyzing approach-avoidance conflict is resolved by outsourcing moral judgment to an internalized authoritative schema, freeing the ego to act.
trigger_scenario: You’re torn whether to blow the whistle on unethical company practices; after consulting your profession’s code of ethics (śāstra) you act decisively, anxiety drops.
anchor_text: Let the śāstra guide your choices, then act without inner conflict.
